title: High-Volume LLMs Under $0.50 in the Compare Prices Grid
description: LLMs at less than $0.50 per 1M input tokens — for the highest-volume production workloads — gridded in TokenRate's Compare Prices.

sections.heading: What "High-Volume Under $0.50" Means in 2026
sections.body: TokenRate's new [Compare Prices grid](/tools/compare-prices) puts every model's per-token rates, context window, and quality score in a single side-by-side view. The point: stop flipping between provider pricing pages and OpenRouter tabs. You pick a provider dropdown, check the models you want, repeat for each provider, and the grid stacks every pick into one comparison table. For workloads at 10B+ tokens/month, even fractional differences in input cost compound to material monthly bill impact. The [Compare Prices view](/tools/compare-prices) makes the "High-Volume Under $0.50" picks visible at a glance — once you've ticked the candidates, the input-cost column does the budget filtering visually. Pair this with [flagship/balanced/fast/reasoning LLM tiers](/blog/flagship-balanced-fast-reasoning-llm-tiers), [Arena AI leaderboard Elo scores explained](/blog/arena-ai-leaderboard-elo-scores-explained), and [how LLM quality scores are calculated](/blog/how-llm-quality-scores-are-calculated).

sections.heading: Who Fits the "High-Volume Under $0.50" Bucket
sections.body: **Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite** (Google): $0.075 / $0.300, Q55, 1M ctx. **GPT-4o mini** (OpenAI): $0.150 / $0.600, Q55, 128K ctx. **Llama 4 Scout** (Meta): $0.200 / $0.600, Q60, 1M ctx. **Mistral Small** (Mistral): $0.200 / $0.600, Q52, 32K ctx. **DeepSeek V3** (DeepSeek): $0.270 / $1.10, Q65, 64K ctx. **Gemini 2.5 Flash** (Google): $0.300 / $2.50, Q68, 1M ctx. **GPT-5 mini** (OpenAI): $0.300 / $2.40, Q70, 128K ctx. **Qwen 2.5 72B** (Alibaba): $0.400 / $1.20, Q60, 32K ctx. All ticked together in [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), the grid lays out the tradeoffs: quality varies from 52 to 70 within the budget, and output costs span $0.300 to $2.50/1M. Pick the highest-Value (quality ÷ input cost) entry that meets your quality floor.

sections.heading: Why Cheapest ≠ Best
sections.body: Within the "High-Volume Under $0.50" bucket, the cheapest model is rarely the best Value pick. A model at $0.20 input / quality 60 has Value = 300; a model at $0.50 input / quality 70 has Value = 140 — the cheaper model wins by 2.1×. But if your workload's quality floor is 65, the cheaper model is disqualified even before Value enters the discussion. Always set the quality floor first, then optimize Value within it. For the framework, see [why the cheapest LLM isn't always the best value](/blog/why-cheapest-llm-isnt-always-best-value).

sections.heading: The High-Volume Under $0.50 Picks, Side-by-Side
sections.body: In [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), tick all candidates across their provider dropdowns. The grid renders input, output, context, and quality in stacked columns. For most High-Volume Under $0.50 workloads, the order of operations is: quality floor first, then Value, then output cost. The grid makes all three visible in one scan. If your monthly volume is small (< 10M tokens), the model differences won't make material bill impact — pick on quality. If volume is high (> 1B tokens/month), even small input-cost deltas compound.

sections.heading: From Budget Bucket to Production
sections.body: Once you've shortlisted within the High-Volume Under $0.50 bucket, run the candidates through [/tools/api-cost-estimator](/tools/api-cost-estimator) with your real workload volume to project monthly cost. For ongoing budget control, instrument token usage in production so you can catch cost regressions early — see [token usage auditing](/blog/token-usage-auditing-find-hidden-costs). Pricing is pulled live from [OpenRouter's models endpoint](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models) and revalidated every 60 minutes via Next.js's incremental cache, so the grid you see is at most an hour stale. Quality scores blend [Arena AI](https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard) Elo with [Artificial Analysis](https://artificialanalysis.ai) intelligence-index data on the same cadence. Run the comparison live at [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), then bookmark the URL for next month's price audit.

faq.question: How do I open the Compare Prices grid?
faq.answer: Two ways: click the 'Compare Prices' tab at the top of the calculator card on the home page, or navigate directly to /tools/compare-prices. The standalone page is also linked from the main navigation under 'Tools'.

faq.question: Can I share my comparison with teammates?
faq.answer: Yes — the page URL captures the current state. Send the link in Slack and your teammate sees the same grid. Useful for procurement and architecture-review meetings.

faq.question: Is the data live or cached?
faq.answer: Live from OpenRouter (prices) and a blended Arena AI + Artificial Analysis pipeline (quality), refreshed on a 60-minute incremental cache. So the grid is at most an hour stale.

faq.question: Where do I go after the grid to project monthly cost?
faq.answer: Once you've picked a winner, go to /tools/api-cost-estimator and plug in the model + your expected monthly token volume. The estimator does the per-1M math against your real workload mix.

ctaText: Run the comparison live at [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), then bookmark the URL for next month's price audit.
